name: Viewport Transform Intent
summary: The shared scale + translate surface model: pan and zoom one content surface (image, map, canvas, diagram, zoomable timeline) under a viewport transform, owning the transform-vocabulary (bounds, focal point, live input axes) — not the gesture recognition or the apply. Keyboard parity + zoom-to-fit/reset are a fixed mechanic, not an add-on. The transform mirror of Viewport Presence's observe.

bounds.description: Whether scale and pan are limited. The exact min/max scale and pan extent are config values the surface (or the platform config it extends) sets; the dimension names the two intents

focal.description: The point a zoom keeps fixed as scale changes

input.description: Which input axes drive the transform. A set, not a single value — any combination is valid. Keyboard is non-optional (it is part of the fixed mechanic below), so it is always live regardless of which pointer axes a surface enables

description:
What Viewport Transform Is

Viewport Transform is the shared scale + translate surface model — the answer to "what is the current pan/zoom of this surface, and how does an input change it?", carried as a viewport transform { translate:[x,y], scale:k }. It is the mirror of Viewport Presence: presence observes a surface's relationship to a frame, transform changes the surface within its frame.

It owns the vocabulary, not the apply — and not the gesture. The reusable middle model is the transform-state plus scaleExtent/translateExtent bounds, the focal point, and keyboard parity — exactly d3-zoom's reusable abstraction. It does not recognize the pinch/pan gesture (it composes Interaction for modality and the Gesture intent for recognition) and it does not own the render mechanism (the transform-state is applied via CSS transform). This intent exists so image-viewer, map, canvas and diagram surfaces compose one transform model instead of each re-implementing clamp + focal + keyboard.

Bounds

Whether scale and pan are limited — the IntersectionObserver-style config knob pair scaleExtent (min/max zoom) and translateExtent (pan extent).

  • unbounded: Zoom and pan freely (no clamp) — the most-permissive default.
  • clamped: Scale is held within scaleExtent and pan within translateExtent; the surface cannot escape its content box.

Focal

The point a zoom keeps fixed as scale changes — the difference between zoom-to-cursor and zoom-to-center.

  • pointer: The point under the cursor/pinch stays put (zoom-to-cursor) — the natural, most-flexible default.
  • center: The surface center stays put.
  • fit: Zoom-to-content — scale and translate so the content fills the viewport (the basis for zoom-to-fit).

Input

Which input axes drive the transform — a set, any combination is valid. touch-action: none on the surface cedes native pinch so the intent can map it.

  • wheel: Wheel/trackpad scroll zooms about the focal point.
  • drag-pan: Pointer drag translates the surface.
  • pinch: Two-finger pinch zooms (recognized by the Gesture intent, mapped to a scale delta here).
  • dblclick: Double-click/tap zooms in a step about the focal point.
  • keyboard: Always live — see the fixed mechanic below.

Fixed mechanic — keyboard + reset (non-negotiable)

Keyboard parity and reset are not a dimension — every conforming surface ships them, mirroring Reorder's keyboard-parity contract. +/- zoom about the center, 0 resets to the identity (or fit) transform, and arrow keys pan. Each transform change is announced to assistive tech. A pointer-only pan/zoom surface is non-conforming.

What this is NOT

Not page-level / browser pinch-zoom — the VisualViewport API is whole-document and read-only. Viewport Transform is element-scoped: it transforms one surface within the page. It is also not CSS zoom (which reflows layout and cannot animate) — the substrate is CSS transform: scale()/translate() (a GPU compositor transform, paint-only, animatable); SVG/canvas surfaces apply the same transform-state to viewBox/context.

Composition

Consumers compose Viewport Transform rather than inline a zoom library: Interaction supplies the input modality, the Gesture intent recognizes pinch/pan, and CSS transform applies the result. This intent owns only the middle model.

Interface Protocol

type TransformBounds = 'unbounded' | 'clamped';type TransformFocal = 'pointer' | 'center' | 'fit';type TransformInput = 'wheel' | 'drag-pan' | 'pinch' | 'dblclick' | 'keyboard';interface ViewportTransform {    // The current surface transform applied via CSS transform: scale()/translate()    translate: [number, number];    scale: number;}interface ViewportTransformIntent {    // Whether scale (scaleExtent) and pan (translateExtent) are clamped    bounds: TransformBounds;    // The point a zoom keeps fixed as scale changes    focal: TransformFocal;    // Which input axes are live (a set); keyboard is always included    input: TransformInput[];}
